title: pinguicula vulgaris var. longifolia
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris var. longifolia, also known as the butterwort or long-leaved butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects in its sticky leaves. Traditionally, Pinguicula vulgaris var. longifolia has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, Pinguicula vulgaris var. longifolia is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
